_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, comprising coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice, is indexed to 2015 at 100 and currently sits at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This rise indicates a significant elevation in the cost of these staple commodities over the period. The climb has been uneven across components and largely concentrated in recent years, influenced by specific market pressures rather than broad-based inflation. These numbers represent global commodity prices tracked by FRED/IMF.
notes.movers: The major price increases driving the basket higher are coffee, which is up 99%, and orange juice, which has risen by 34% since 2015. Wheat and sugar prices have increased more modestly, by 19% and 13% respectively. These shifts are attributable primarily to weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee beans and citrus greening impacting orange juice production—in thin, specialized soft commodity markets. This indicates that these price changes stem from supply-side disruptions rather than broad economic inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely driven by a few weather-sensitive and disease-affected crops. Given the nature of these agricultural challenges, commodity prices can remain elevated for extended periods as recovery and adaptation to adverse conditions take time. This underscores the vulnerability of soft commodities to climatic and biological factors.
